announcement.html: Easter Opening Hours Chambers will be closed from 5pm on Thursday 2 April until 9am on Tuesday 7 April. If your enquiry is extremely urgent, the out of hours number is 0191 245 0110.

barristers.name: Richard Blackwood
barristers.bio: Richard is one of the leading silks on the Northern Circuit in Administrative and Public Law. He has appeared in the Supreme Court, Court of Appeal and High Court in numerous landmark judicial review cases. His practice covers all aspects of public law including local government, planning, education, and human rights.

barristers.name: David Morrison
barristers.bio: David is a highly regarded silk with a broad commercial practice encompassing company law, shareholder disputes, banking and finance, insolvency and professional negligence. He is known for his strategic approach and tenacious advocacy.

barristers.name: James Henderson
barristers.bio: James is recognised as one of the leading employment silks in the North of England. He acts for both employers and employees in the Employment Tribunal, EAT and appellate courts, with particular expertise in discrimination, whistleblowing and injunctive relief.

barristers.name: Michael Foster
barristers.bio: Michael is a highly experienced criminal silk with over 25 years of practice in the most serious and complex criminal cases. He is regularly instructed in murder, serious fraud, drug conspiracies and cases of national importance.

barristers.name: Sarah Mitchell
barristers.bio: Sarah is a leading criminal silk with particular expertise in cases involving serious sexual offences, historic abuse, and complex multi-defendant cases. She is known for her careful preparation and commanding court presence.

barristers.name: Thomas Clarke
barristers.bio: Thomas is one of the most experienced personal injury and clinical negligence silks on the circuit. He is regularly instructed in catastrophic injury claims and complex clinical negligence cases involving significant financial value.

barristers.name: Helen Hogben
barristers.bio: Helen has a busy employment law practice acting predominantly for claimants in cases of discrimination, whistleblowing and unfair dismissal. She is particularly known for her expertise in disability discrimination and equality law.

barristers.name: Robert Davies
barristers.bio: Robert is an experienced planning barrister regularly appearing at planning inquiries, hearings and in the High Court. He acts for developers, landowners and local planning authorities across a wide range of development types.

barristers.name: Andrew Patterson
barristers.bio: Andrew has a broad property practice covering landlord and tenant, boundary disputes, easements, covenants, co-ownership and all aspects of residential and commercial property litigation.

barristers.name: Catherine Hughes
barristers.bio: Catherine specialises in public law children cases, representing local authorities, parents and children. She is regularly instructed in complex care proceedings and cases involving non-accidental injury.

barristers.name: Rebecca Walsh
barristers.bio: Rebecca has a busy family finance practice, acting in financial remedy proceedings following divorce. She is experienced in cases involving business assets, trusts, pensions and international elements.

barristers.name: Laura Jenkins
barristers.bio: Laura is a specialist Court of Protection practitioner acting in welfare and property/financial affairs proceedings involving adults who lack mental capacity.

barristers.name: Priya Sharma
barristers.bio: Priya has developed a strong employment law practice acting for both claimants and respondents across all areas of employment law with particular expertise in race, sex and disability discrimination.

barristers.name: Emma Thompson
barristers.bio: Emma has a busy immigration practice covering asylum, human rights and deportation appeals. She is regularly instructed in complex cases before the First-tier and Upper Tribunals.

barristers.name: Jason Patel
barristers.bio: Jason acts in a wide range of immigration matters including asylum, leave to remain, deportation and nationality cases. He is known for his thorough preparation and clear written submissions.

barristers.name: Sophie Williams
barristers.bio: Sophie has a developing practice in education law, acting for schools, local authorities and parents in exclusion appeals, SEN matters and employment disputes in the education sector.

news.title: Trinity's Pupil Barristers Start Second Six of Pupillage
news.excerpt: Trinity Chambers' current pupil barristers will shortly be accepting instructions on their own cases as they commence the second half of their pupillage.

news.title: Managing Cancer in the Workplace: A Legal Guide for Employers
news.excerpt: Helen Hogben examines the legal obligations employers face when an employee receives a cancer diagnosis, including reasonable adjustments and disability discrimination.

news.title: The Sentencing Council: New Guidelines for Serious Violence Offences
news.excerpt: Michael Foster KC reviews the Sentencing Council's updated guidelines for serious violence, exploring their impact on sentencing outcomes.

news.title: Asylum Seekers and the Safety of Third Countries: Post-Rwanda Update
news.excerpt: Emma Thompson provides an update on the legal landscape for asylum seekers following the Supreme Court ruling and subsequent legislative changes.

news.title: Fundamental Dishonesty: Recent Developments and Practical Implications
news.excerpt: Thomas Clarke KC analyses recent court decisions on fundamental dishonesty under s.57 of the Criminal Justice and Courts Act 2015.

news.title: The Planning and Infrastructure Bill: Key Changes for Developers
news.excerpt: Robert Davies examines the most significant provisions of the Planning and Infrastructure Bill 2025 and what they mean for developers.
